auth.createAccountDesc: Set the email and password that will protect this dashboard.
auth.signInDesc: Sign in to manage your keys, routing, and analytics.
auth.serverUnreachable: Can't reach the server. Make sure the backend is running ({{cmd}}).

keys.title: Keys
keys.description: Provider credentials and the unified API key your apps connect with.
keys.unifiedKeyDesc: Use this as your OpenAI api_key; it authenticates requests to this proxy.
keys.serverError: Can't reach the server on {{url}}. Make sure the backend is running. {{cmd}} starts both, and the server logs print under the {{prefix}} prefix.
keys.embeddingsHint: (model: "auto" or a family from the Embeddings tab)
keys.proxyDesc: Route outbound LLM requests through a proxy. Supports SOCKS5, HTTP, and HTTPS.
keys.proxyEnvHint: Also configurable via the {{code}} environment variable (takes precedence). Leave blank to disable. Examples:
keys.customDesc: Point at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint: llama.cpp, LM Studio, vLLM, a local Ollama, or a remote gateway. List one model per line (or comma-separated) to add several at once; they all share the one endpoint. The API key is optional (most local servers don't need one).
keys.keylessHint: No API key needed: this provider's free tier is anonymous (rate-limited per IP).
keys.label: Label
keys.noKeysYet: No provider keys yet. Add one above to start routing.

fallback.title: Models
fallback.description: Pick a routing strategy. In Manual mode you drag to set the order; the other strategies route by live score across reliability, speed and intelligence.
fallback.manualHint: Manual mode: requests follow the order below, top-to-bottom. Drag to reorder.
fallback.scoreHint: Scores update from live traffic. The order below is how requests are routed right now.
fallback.noModels: No models available. Add API keys on the <link>Keys page</link> first.
fallback.customWeightsDesc: Sliders are independent; shares auto-balance to 100%.
fallback.weightAboveZero: At least one weight must be above zero.
fallback.toolsTitle: Emits structured tool calls, so it is eligible for tool-bearing requests

table.guardrailsTip: Always-on guardrails: free-quota headroom × live rate-limit penalty. Below 1.0 means the model is being held back.
table.scoreTip: Final routing score = weighted average of the three axes, multiplied by the guardrails. Higher routes first.

priority.label: Manual
priority.blurb: Route in the exact order you set below. Drag the handles to reorder. No scoring; the chain is followed top-to-bottom.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.blurb: Reliability leads (50%), with speed and intelligence weighted equally (25% each). A sensible all-round default.

smartest.label: Smartest
smartest.blurb: Prefer the most capable model that still works. Intelligence 55%, reliability 35%, speed 10%.

fastest.label: Fastest
fastest.blurb: Prefer the fastest model that still works. Speed 55%, reliability 35%, intelligence 10%.

reliable.label: Most reliable
reliable.blurb: Maximize success rate above all. Reliability 70%, speed and intelligence 15% each.

custom.label: Custom
custom.blurb: Set your own balance of reliability, speed and intelligence with sliders. Same engine as the presets, just your weights.

embeddings.title: Models
embeddings.description: Embeddings fail over within a family only: the same model served by another provider. Vectors from different models are incompatible, so the router never swaps models on you.
embeddings.autoHint: {{model}} on {{endpoint}} routes to the default family. Naming a family (or a provider model id) pins that family; providers inside it are tried in order.

playground.title: Playground
playground.description: Send a chat completion through the router and see which provider serves it.
playground.noModelsHint: No models yet. Add an API key on the Keys page to populate this list.
playground.emptyTitle: Send a message to get started.
playground.emptyDesc: Using {{model}}. Switch models in the selector above.
playground.placeholder: Type a message… (⏎ to send, ⇧⏎ for newline)

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Request volume, latency, token usage, and failures.
analytics.savingsHintActual: You actually saved ${{amount}} over the last {{range}}. That is what the same tokens would have cost on paid APIs, priced per model.
analytics.savingsHintProjected: The number shown projects your pace from the last {{span}} of data to a full 30 days.
analytics.savingsHintReal: The number shown is your real 30-day total.
analytics.requestsHintPinned: {{pinned}} of these requests pinned a specific model by name. {{honored}} were served by the pinned model; {{failed}} failed over to a different one. The rest were auto-routed.
analytics.requestsHintAuto: All requests in this period were auto-routed; no client pinned a specific model by name.

premium.title: Premium
premium.description: The live model catalog, on every device.
premium.liveDesc: New free models, quota changes, and quirk fixes land here within hours of being shipped. The app checks automatically twice a day; nothing to do.
premium.freeDesc: Free tier: the catalog refreshes from a monthly snapshot, checked automatically twice a day. Premium switches this to the live feed, updated every 2-3 days.
premium.cancelAtEnd: Will not renew. Premium until {{date}}, then this install falls back to the free monthly catalog.
premium.inactiveDesc: This key is no longer active. The app keeps working on the free monthly catalog.
premium.stripeHint: Manage subscription opens Stripe's billing portal: cancel, update your card, or download invoices. Removing the key only deactivates this device; your purchase is untouched.
premium.recoverHint: Your key is in your purchase email and on the post-checkout page. Lost it?
premium.upsellDesc: New free models the moment they exist, instead of on the 1st of the month. One key, every device. Cancel anytime; the router stays free forever.
